title: First out-of-box OSS product — universality vs domain depth
note: Decision: which single artifact to open (AGPL) so a stranger can run it in minutes and appreciate it, while the SLM weights + filled 404-mask fleet stay classified (FP-009). Axes graded honestly: external-adoption counts are projections (E/G); 'already exists + deterministic' is measured (M).

options.name: Vivechana CLI (npx vivechana — universal decision engine)

I.anchor: between one-desk(5) and broad: every ANKR decision already runs through it + any external decision-maker; external count is a projection

L.anchor: unlocks 'score don't argue' + check-back bets downstream across any org; below bitmask binary-proof(9-10)

R.anchor: approaching Forja-STATE reach — domain-agnostic, agents can call it, propagates to any partner/org

Rep.anchor: bitmask trie/hook any-domain-no-code(9-10): deterministic CLI, runs on decision #1000 with zero new code — the OSS ideal

F.anchor: exists/trivial — vivechana.mjs already runs; packaging as npx is trivial

I.anchor: AI devs building agents — sizable external audience; projection

L.anchor: unlocks proof-gated agency downstream for any agent project

R.anchor: propagates across agent/LLM ecosystem, below universal substrate reach

Rep.anchor: reusable harness across projects, near hook-level but some per-project wiring

I.anchor: a primitive — narrow direct out-of-box appreciation; you must build around it to feel value

L.anchor: near bitmask binary-proof — very high downstream leverage once adopted

R.anchor: propagates as a protocol across services/agents

Rep.anchor: pure encoding — fully replicable, no per-domain code

F.anchor: needs packaging + worked-example docs to be usable standalone

I.anchor: one-desk(5): service operators; solo dev with 1-2 services gets modest out-of-box value

R.anchor: propagates to ops + agents, needs a fleet to show full reach

Rep.anchor: works across services but needs per-host config — below pure-hook

options.name: ComplyMitra OSS edition (domain depth — Indian CA compliance)

I.anchor: narrow niche — Indian CAs; deep appreciation but small direct count

L.anchor: domain-specific, low downstream leverage to other services

R.anchor: one-service surfaces(5) or lower — does not propagate across the universe

Rep.anchor: manual per-jurisdiction — kills replicability; each country re-done

F.anchor: carving an OSS edition out of the gated product is real work + conflicts FP-009 (product gated, not OSS)
